params.prompt: Transform this room image into a cinematic editorial real-estate still. Preserve exact architecture, perspective, walls, windows, door openings, furniture layout, lens realism, and room scale. Maintain believable residential scale for Taste (kitchen) at roughly medium. Medium light derived from the adjacent dining bay; emphasize practical pendant lights over prep surfaces in evening variants. Keep reflections muted—matte materials preferred. Calm, textured Japandi interiors that balance Scandinavian lightness with Japanese restraint. Warm neutrals, light oak, and natural textiles create an uncluttered, family-friendly environment. Prioritize a cozy standing-desk work corner near natural light, low-profile furniture, and subtle plant accents. Preserve all measured architecture and floorplan flow—styling should enhance the existing layout rather than restructure it. Keep these fixed unless the user explicitly wants transformation: exact architecture room geometry furniture layout lens realism vertical line integrity believable scale light entering from visible sources only VISUAL CONSISTENCY REQUIREMENT (reference image provided): The Dine (dining) visible through the open pass-through or doorway must show exactly the same dining table, pendant light above the dining area, dining chairs, round oak table, woven rattan pendant, three-panel bay windows, minimal ceramic vignette, 4 linen chairs, minimal ceramic tableware as in the reference image — do not invent different furniture. The kitchen cabinets, range hood, kitchen island or counter, open shelving with ceramics, visible pendant or practical lighting, clear sightline to dining bay, wood cutting board or bowl, visible pendant or under-cabinet practical light in this room must exactly match the style, materials, and form shown in reference image — treat it as the definitive source of truth for these pieces. Previous failure modes to correct: wrong_room_type, object_missing. The image depicts a dining area rather than a kitchen, failing to capture the required kitchen elements. Essential objects like the open shelving and practical lighting over prep areas are missing, impacting room correctness and object completeness.
